playbook_gen1:
# Forecasting Playbook — Generation 1 (naive seed) — GitHub issue closure

> DOMAIN 2: "Will this GitHub issue be closed within 30 days of being opened?"
> This is a FRESH gen-1 playbook for a domain the engine has never seen. The
> orchestration, verifier, and diagnostician are UNCHANGED from domain 1 — only
> this seed doctrine and the dataset differ. Rules are applied to the question
> TEXT plus the dataset's leak-proof at-open structural fields (`category` = repo,
> `ladder_pos_bin` = body-length bucket, `is_deadline`, `direction`, `ladder_rung`,
> `trivial_bound`). Zero web research; later generations only add calibration
> learned from the TRAIN miss distribution, each rule citing its generation.

## Rules (human-readable)

### R1 — Coin-flip prior per repository
Classify each issue by its `category` (the repo it belongs to) and predict
**p = 0.50** for every repo.
*Origin: gen-1 seed. With no doctrine, the coin flip is the honest baseline
(Brier 0.25). Repos are kept as separate types so the diagnostician can later
learn each repo's distinct 30-day closure base rate from TRAIN.*

### R2 — Default
Anything not matching a known repo: predict **p = 0.50**.
*Origin: gen-1 seed.*

## Machine-readable contract
The forecaster classifies each question into the FIRST matching type in
`classification_order`, then assigns `probability`. If the type has
`probability_by_posbin` (a 5-element array) it uses `probability_by_posbin[ladder_pos_bin]`;
else if it has `probability_by_rung` it uses `probability_by_rung[ladder_rung]`.
Output per question:
`{id, type, probability, base_rate_used, key_evidence, what_would_change_my_mind}`.

```json
{
  "generation": 1,
  "default_probability": 0.50,
  "classification_order": ["kubernetes", "react", "rust", "default"],
  "types": {
    "kubernetes": {
      "definition": "category == 'kubernetes'.",
      "probability": 0.50,
      "origin_gen": 1,
      "evidence": "naive seed prior"
    },
    "react": {
      "definition": "category == 'react'.",
      "probability": 0.50,
      "origin_gen": 1,
      "evidence": "naive seed prior"
    },
    "rust": {
      "definition": "category == 'rust'.",
      "probability": 0.50,
      "origin_gen": 1,
      "evidence": "naive seed prior"
    },
    "default": {
      "definition": "catch-all: category is not a known repo.",
      "probability": 0.50,
      "origin_gen": 1,
      "evidence": "naive seed prior"
    }
  }
}
```
playbook_final:
PLAYBOOK GENERATION 5 - GitHub issue closure

Gen 5 no-op: no remaining bias (rust gap 0.014, react gap 0.0, kubernetes gap 0.0); posbin curves applied (react gen-2, kubernetes gen-3); rust candidate null; gap within noise. No web research, no holdout.

R1 Per-repo prior (gen-1, retained). Gen 5: no change, all repos calibrated (rust 0.014, react 0.0, kubernetes 0.0).
R2 react posbin (gen-2, retained). Gen 2: 0.50 but resolved 0.231 n=52 gap 0.269, isotonic candidate. Gen 5: 0.231 vs 0.231 n=52 gap 0.0, retained.
R3 rust flat 0.376 (gen-2, retained). Gen 2: 0.50 but resolved 0.362 n=47 gap 0.138, 0.362 plus 10pct shrinkage = 0.376. Gen 5: gap 0.014, candidate null (raw [0.333,0.714,0,0.625,0.417] non-monotone), within noise so not over-shrunk, retained.
R4 kubernetes posbin (gen-3, retained). Gen 3: flat 0.50 masked slope (empirics [0.538,0.571,0.4,0.583,0.333] n=[13,7,5,12,12]), isotonic candidate. Gen 5: 0.49 vs 0.49 n=49 gap 0.0, retained.
R5 Default 0.50 (gen-1, retained). Gen 5: no new-type evidence, retained.

Contract: first matching type in classification_order; use probability_by_posbin[ladder_pos_bin] if present else flat probability.

JSON contract generation 5:
{"generation":5,"default_probability":0.50,"classification_order":["kubernetes","react","rust","default"],"types":{"kubernetes":{"definition":"category=='kubernetes'.","probability_by_posbin":[0.567,0.53,0.492,0.454,0.417],"origin_gen":3,"evidence":"gen 3 isotonic from masked slope; gen 5 gap 0.0 retained"},"react":{"definition":"category=='react'.","probability_by_posbin":[0.292,0.251,0.209,0.168,0.127],"origin_gen":2,"evidence":"gen 2 isotonic from 0.269 gap; gen 5 gap 0.0 retained"},"rust":{"definition":"category=='rust'.","probability":0.376,"origin_gen":2,"evidence":"gen 2 shrink to 0.376; gen 5 gap 0.014 candidate null retained"},"default":{"definition":"catch-all.","probability":0.50,"origin_gen":1,"evidence":"gen-1 coin-flip; gen 5 no new-type evidence retained"}}}

verifier_systematic_errors: type react: predicts ~0.50 but resolves ~0.23 over 52 items -> over-predicting
type rust: predicts ~0.50 but resolves ~0.36 over 47 items -> over-predicting
diagnoses.diagnostician_analysis: react and rust biggest biases; kubernetes well-calibrated, untouched.

verifier_systematic_errors: type rust: predicts ~0.376 but resolves ~0.362 over 47 items -> marginally over-predicting (largest weighted gap, but negligible)
type kubernetes: predicts ~0.500 but resolves ~0.490 over 49 items -> marginally over-predicting (negligible)
errors_addressed: kubernetes used a flat 0.50 that ignored its monotone intra-ladder slope: TRAIN posbin empirics [0.538,0.571,0.4,0.583,0.333] (n=[13,7,5,12,12]) show higher strikes resolve YES less often; replaced flat prob with isotonic monotone_candidate [0.567,0.53,0.492,0.454,0.417] to cut within-bin Brier without breaking the well-calibrated 0.49 aggregate
diagnoses.diagnostician_analysis:
TRAIN calibration shows all three known types are already well-calibrated at the aggregate level after gen-2's changes: rust predicted 0.376 vs resolve 0.362 (gap 0.014), kubernetes 0.50 vs 0.49 (gap 0.01), react 0.231 vs 0.231 (gap 0.0). The gen-2 fixes landed. So there is no remaining aggregate-level type bias to chase.

The only remaining systematic, generalizable improvement available is at the posbin level for the kubernetes threshold type. kubernetes currently uses a single flat 0.50 with gap 0.01 aggregate. But by_type_posbin shows a non-trivial monotone structure that the flat number masks: empirical bins [0.538,0.571,0.4,0.583,0.333] over n=[13,7,5,12,12], and the provided isotonic monotone_candidate is [0.567,0.53,0.492,0.454,0.417]. This is the allowed move for a THRESHOLD type (direction=='above'): replace flat probability with the monotone_candidate array, which encodes "higher strike in ladder => less likely YES" and generalizes to holdout rather than overfitting the noisy raw bins (note bin2 n=5 dips to 0.4, bin4 drops to 0.333 — raw is noisy, isotonic smooths it). The aggregate of the monotone candidate stays near 0.49-0.50 so it does not break the already-good aggregate calibration; it just reduces Brier within bins by predicting the high-strike bins lower (0.417) and low-strike bins higher (0.567). This is the single biggest remaining systematic bias: kubernetes's intra-ladder slope being ignored by a flat number.

I considered re-shrinking rust or react further, but both are within ~0.01-0.014 of empirical — moving them would be over-tuning noise and the instructions forbid changing well-calibrated types and warn against over-shrinking. rust's monotone_candidate is null (non-monotone empirics), so no posbin move is valid there. react already uses its monotone_candidate and is perfect. So I make exactly ONE change: kubernetes flat -> monotone_candidate posbin curve. Everything else is retained verbatim with its prior evidence citations. Default stays 0.50 (no evidence). Generation bumped to 3.

verifier_systematic_errors: type rust: predicts ~0.376 but resolves ~0.362 over 47 items -> marginally over-predicting (largest weighted gap, but negligible)
type kubernetes: predicts ~0.490 but resolves ~0.490 over 49 items -> essentially perfectly calibrated in aggregate, no systematic bias
errors_addressed: rust gap 0.014, react 0.0, kubernetes 0.0 all calibrated
monotone candidates applied react gen2 kubernetes gen3, rust null
diagnoses.diagnostician_analysis: All TRAIN types calibrated. Shrink and monotone-swap levers exhausted (react gen2, kubernetes gen3, rust candidate null). No new-type evidence. Gen 4 is a no-op retaining all rules; changing calibrated types would raise Brier.

verifier_systematic_errors: type rust: predicts ~0.38 but resolves ~0.36 over 47 items -> slightly over-predicting (largest |gap|*n, but gap is negligible at 0.014)

type kubernetes: predicts ~0.49 but resolves ~0.49 over 49 items -> essentially perfectly calibrated in aggregate (no systematic bias); the high overall Brier (0.217) stems from low resolution/sharpness, not directional bias
errors_addressed: rust candidate null, no new-type evidence, no-op
diagnoses.diagnostician_analysis: No valid move remains (rust gap 0.014 within noise; react/kubernetes calibrated with posbin applied; rust candidate null; no new-type evidence). Gen 5 is a no-op.

leakage.statement: Zero outbound research calls during the backtest — leak-proof by construction.
